subject: sa-hb1
message: Build customer support resolution agent per LAB_PROMPT.md and ERRATA.md

payload.title: Build customer support resolution agent per LAB_PROMPT.md and ERRATA.md
payload.description: Read LAB_PROMPT.md and ERRATA.md in this directory first. Build a working CLI agent with: sharply-scoped tools (get_customer, lookup_order, process_refund, escalate_to_human), all four error categories (transient/validation/business/permission), three valid escalation triggers (customer requests human, policy exception, inability to make progress — NOT self-reported confidence), context summarization at handoff only, and a programmatic prerequisite blocking process_refund until get_customer has verified the customer. Include mock backend with edge cases, run.sh entry point, and sample invocations. Python.

subject: sa-2rm
message: Review: Rig 01 customer support agent — independent code review

payload.title: Review: Rig 01 customer support agent — independent code review
payload.description: Independent review of the customer support agent built by sa-hb1. Review against LAB_PROMPT.md requirements and ERRATA.md corrections. Check: all four error categories present, three valid escalation triggers (NOT self-reported confidence), programmatic prerequisite enforcement, tool description quality, handoff packet completeness, mock backend edge cases. Produce structured findings as JSON. This is a SEPARATE session from the builder — session isolation per CCA-F Domain 4.6.

subject: sa-e2p
message: Iterate: Rig 01 v2 — address review findings from sa-2rm

payload.title: Iterate: Rig 01 v2 — address review findings from sa-2rm
payload.description: Second build iteration of the customer support agent. Take findings from the independent review (sa-2rm), create a new GC session (session isolation), and improve the app. Track what changed between v1 and v2. This exercises the iterative refinement pattern (CCA-F Domain 3.5) and session isolation (Domain 4.6).

Build a working CLI agent with: sharply-scoped tools (get_customer, lookup_order, process_refund, escalate_to_human), all four error categories (transient/validation/business/permission), three valid escalation triggers (customer requests human, policy exception, inability to make progress — NOT self-reported confidence), context summarization at handoff only, and a programmatic prerequisite blocking process_refund until get_customer has verified the customer. payload.title: mol-do-work
payload.description:
Simple work formula — read the bead, do what it says, close it.

This is the minimal work lifecycle for coding agents. No git branching,
no worktree isolation, no refinery handoff. The agent reads the bead's
description, implements the solution in the current working directory,
and closes the bead when done.

Use this for demos and simple single-agent workflows. For production
multi-agent setups with branch isolation and merge review, use
mol-polecat-work instead.

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | caller | The work bead ID assigned to this agent |
